target.title: test(plugins): cover Link agent wallet bundle shape
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Stripe Link agent wallet now publishes a Codex-style bundle with skills and an MCP server, but OpenClaw had no compatibility guardrail for that exact shape.
- Why it matters: Agent-wallet plugins are a likely near-term install path, and regressions in bundle detection, MCP loading, skill metadata parsing, or MCP tool names would make Link fail quietly or expose the wrong tool names.
- What changed: Added focused tests for Link-style Codex bundle manifest detection, enabled bundle MCP loading, YAML skill metadata parsing, and MCP tool-name generation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No runtime behavior, payment flow, credentials handling, or Link-specific core logic changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Link-style bundle/MCP fixture only
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Create a Codex bundle fixture with .codex-plugin/plugin.json, skills, and .mcp.json pointing at pnpx @stripe/link-cli --mcp.
2. Load enabled bundle MCP config for that fixture.
3. Parse Link-style skill YAML frontmatter with metadata.openclaw install hints.
4. Generate MCP tool names for Link server tools.

### Expected

- The manifest is detected as a Codex bundle with skills and MCP capabilities.
- The enabled MCP config loads the link server with the expected command, args, and plugin-root cwd.
- Skill metadata preserves the Link homepage, required bin, and node install hint.
- MCP tool names keep the bundle server prefix.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior in the focused tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw should recognize a Link-style Codex bundle as a bundle with `skills` and `mcpServers`, load the enabled Link MCP server from `.mcp.json`, parse the Link skill metadata install hints, and keep generated MCP tool names under the `link__` server prefix.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at `f6f0bec9a518dd4c364937e79d218674536b9863`, macOS host, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 10.33.2, temporary OpenClaw HOME/workspace containing a Link-style Codex bundle fixture under the real plugin discovery path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a `pnpm exec tsx -e` proof script that wrote `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, and a Link-style `SKILL.md`, then called OpenClaw production bundle manifest detection, enabled bundle MCP loading, skill frontmatter metadata parsing, and MCP tool-name generation.
- Evidence after fix:

```
format=codex
manifest_ok=true
capabilities=skills,mcpServers
mcp_diagnostics=0
mcp_command=pnpx
mcp_args=@stripe/link-cli --mcp
mcp_cwd_realpath_is_plugin_root=true
skill_bin=link-cli
skill_install=node:@stripe/link-cli
tool_separator=__
tool_names=link__auth_login,link__spend-request_create
```

- Observed result after fix: the Link-style bundle is detected as Codex, exposes the expected skills/MCP capabilities, loads the Link MCP server without diagnostics, resolves cwd to the plugin root, preserves the node install hint for `@stripe/link-cli`, and generates stable `link__...` MCP tool names.
- What was not tested: live Link auth, live spend requests, or real payment credential retrieval.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Focused Link-style bundle, MCP config, skill metadata, and tool-name tests.
- Edge cases checked: Bundle server prefix with underscore and hyphenated tool names; node install metadata parsing.
- What you did not verify: Live Link auth, live spend requests, or actual payment credential retrieval.

Commands run:

- pnpm docs:list
- pnpm test src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-names.test.ts src/agents/skills/frontmatter.test.ts src/plugins/bundle-manifest.test.ts src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-names.test.ts src/agents/skills/frontmatter.test.ts src/plugins/bundle-manifest.test.ts src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test-support.ts src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
- pnpm exec tsx -e '<temporary Link-style bundle proof>'

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

None.
